sections.paragraph:
ἡδυπάθεια, ας, ἡ (ἡδύς + πάθη, ‘experience of someth. pleasant’; X., Cyr. 7, 5, 74; Cebes 9, 3; 28, 1; Plut., Mor. 132c; 4 Macc 2:2, 4) experience of a luxurious mode of life, enjoyment, comfort; pl. (Athen. 4, 165e) ἀποτάσσεσθαι ταῖς ἡδ. renounce the enjoyments 2 Cl 16:2. μισεῖν τὰς ἡδ. τῆς ψυχῆς hate the evil pleasures of the soul 17:7. Cp. DELG s.v. ἥδομαι B.

someth. someth. = something

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Cebes Cebes , prominent in Platonic dialogues; ref. is to a dialogue associated with his name, I a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. pl. = plural

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 435
